problem: Why does the immune system attack the body's own tissue in autoimmune disease?
hypothesis: Why does the immune system attack the body's own tissue in autoimmune disease? remains unsolved because previous research missed the role of Telomerase. Telomerase activation/inhibition in target cells directly addresses this.

experiment.protocol: Human organoid model with 15 month follow-up. Control group receives placebo/sham.

wisdom: Real knowledge is to know the extent of one's ignorance. — Confucius
